claim: YOLO harness boundary audit implementation exists in src/demand/evidence-dispatch.ts and __tests__/demand-evidence-dispatch.test.ts with git-backed and non-git file system coverage.

evidence.summary: gitFiles() runs git ls-files -co --exclude-standard -z to capture tracked, cached, and other files
evidence.why: This is the primary git-backed boundary detection mechanism.

evidence.summary: walkFiles() provides recursive filesystem walk fallback with directory exclusions
evidence.why: This is the non-git boundary detection fallback.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot() combines git and walk results, excludes allowed roots, computes sha256 digests
evidence.why: This is the core boundary snapshot logic.

evidence.summary: Git and walk are combined via Set union: git ? [...new Set([...git, ...walkFiles(projectRoot])] : walkFiles(projectRoot)
evidence.why: This proves git ls-files and walk are combined, not replaced.

evidence.summary: Allowed roots are excluded: if (allowed.some((root) => isWithin(absolute, root))) continue;
evidence.why: This is the artifact root exclusion mechanism.
These are the standard directories excluded from boundary snapshots.

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots() compares before/after maps and returns added/modified/deleted changes
evidence.why: This is the mutation detection logic.

evidence.summary: git-backed boundary detects untracked project mutations test
evidence.why: This proves git-backed boundary detection works for untracked files.

evidence.summary: Assert verifies untracked-mutation.txt is detected as added: assert.ok(result.boundary.changes.some((change) => change.path === 'untracked-mutation.txt' && change.change === 'added'))
evidence.why: This proves untracked file detection is tested.

evidence.summary: git-backed boundary detects gitignored project mutations outside excluded dirs test
evidence.why: This proves gitignored file detection works.

evidence.summary: Assert verifies .env (gitignored) is detected as added
evidence.why: This proves gitignored file detection is tested.

evidence.summary: non-git boundary fallback detects modified project files and ignores artifacts test
evidence.why: This proves non-git boundary detection works and excludes artifacts.

evidence.summary: Assert verifies artifacts are excluded: assert.equal(result.boundary.changes.some((change) => change.path.includes('agent-scratch.txt')), false)
evidence.why: This proves artifact root exclusion is tested.

evidence.summary: runDemandEvidenceDispatchRuntime() builds boundaryBefore snapshot, runs agents, builds boundaryAfter, computes changes, and emits BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers
evidence.why: This is the full boundary enforcement workflow.

evidence.summary: boundary result includes project_mutation violated/clean status, allowed_write_roots, and detailed changes list
evidence.why: This is the boundary audit output format.

evidence.summary: inspectDemandTriage() returns evidence_policy cross_check for technical risk/high cost, single_agent for existing projects, none for greenfield
evidence.why: This is the evidence policy routing logic.

evidence.summary: Route selection: technicalRisk || highCost || acceptanceMissingForPrd || unclearApprovalForPrd triggers careful route, otherwise fast
evidence.why: This is the routing trigger logic.

evidence.summary: Current handoff docs exist under docs/memory/
evidence.why: This is the project memory root location.

assumptions: The git ls-files -co flags capture tracked, cached, and other files as documented by git
The dispatch artifact root is always excluded via allowed roots parameter
Full-tool agent stress testing with real Claude -p agents would expose any bypass vectors
risks: A full-tool agent with file write capabilities could bypass boundary if artifact root exclusion fails
Dirty working tree edge cases may not be fully covered by current tests
missing: Stress test evidence using real Claude -p agents with full tools against actual YOLO projects
Verification that dispatch artifact root path is always correctly passed to buildBoundarySnapshot allowedRoots parameter
Evidence of boundary audit behavior in dirty working trees with staged, unstaged, and untracked changes

result.notes: Implementation exists with git-backed (ls-files -co) and non-git (walk) coverage. Tests prove untracked and gitignored detection plus artifact exclusion. Stress testing with real full-tool Claude agents is the primary gap.
